en.extract:
The Javan heleia (Heleia javanica), also known as the Javan grey-throated white-eye, grey-throated ibon, Javan heleia or Mees's white-eye, is a species of bird in the family Zosteropidae. It is endemic to Java and Bali.

The '''Javan heleia''' ('''''Heleia javanica'''''), also known as the '''Javan grey-throated white-eye''', '''grey-throated ibon''', '''Javan heleia''' or '''Mees's white-eye''',<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/new/ioc-lists/master-list-2/ |title=Master Lists: v15.1 |website=[[IOC World Bird List]] |publisher=[[International Ornithologists' Union]] |access-date=13 March 2025}}</ref> is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Zosteropidae]]. It is [[Endemism|endemic]] to [[Java]] and [[Bali]].
